Human.weaknessBias: Restlessness. Humans may push too hard because they feel time, opportunity or legacy slipping away.

Elf.weaknessBias: Detachment. Elves may observe for too long before acting, especially when mortal urgency feels distant to them.

Dwarf.weaknessBias: Stubbornness. Dwarves may hold to a promise, grudge or tradition even when changing course would be wiser.

Halfling.weaknessBias: Overcommitment. Halflings may put themselves in danger because they cannot ignore someone who needs help.

Gnome.weaknessBias: Obsession. Gnomes may chase an idea, mystery or invention long after caution says to stop.

Tiefling.weaknessBias: Defensiveness. Tieflings may expect rejection before it happens and push away people who might have stood beside them.

Dragonborn.weaknessBias: Pride. Dragonborn may struggle to retreat, apologise or accept being seen as weak.

Half-Elf.weaknessBias: Uncertainty. Half-Elves may question where they truly belong, especially when forced to choose sides.

Half-Orc.weaknessBias: Anger. Half-Orcs may react strongly when mocked, cornered or treated like a monster.

Aasimar.weaknessBias: Burden. Aasimar may believe they must be better than everyone else expects, even when they are breaking inside.

Goblin.weaknessBias: Distrust. Goblins may assume every offer has a hook and every kindness hides a trap.

Orc.weaknessBias: Impatience. Orcs may act quickly because waiting can feel like weakness, hesitation or wasted survival time.

Goliath.weaknessBias: Competitiveness. Goliaths may turn danger into a test of worth, even when caution would save them.
